masthead.title_html: Reason Your Way Through the Pantry
masthead.title_plain: Reason Your Way Through the Pantry
masthead.dek: Twenty questions on crops, cuisines, drinks, and ingredients. Every answer sits in a set you can list out loud.
masthead.footer_html: Figures verified against FAO data, industry trackers, and reference sources, June 2026.

questions.q: Which country grows more coffee than any other, producing more than a third of the world's supply?
questions.detail: Brazil produced about 64.7 million 60-kg bags in 2024, roughly 38% of global output.
questions.why: One country alone covers nearly 4 of every 10 bags of coffee on Earth.
questions.more: Vietnam is a distant second at about 29 million bags (17%).
questions.source: Visual Capitalist, Visualizing Global Coffee Production in 2024

questions.q: Leaving out South America, which country grows the most coffee in the world?
questions.detail: Vietnam produces about 29 million bags a year, nearly all of it robusta, and is the world's No. 2 producer overall.
questions.why: The reflex answer is a South American name, but the biggest non-South-American grower is in Southeast Asia, at about 17% of world output.
questions.more: Vietnam alone supplies over 40% of the world's robusta beans.
questions.source: Asia News Network, Vietnam cements status as world's No.2 coffee producer

questions.q: By share of all meat eaten worldwide, which single type of meat is eaten the most?
questions.detail: Pork makes up about 36% of global meat consumption, ahead of chicken at about 33% and beef at about 24%.
questions.why: Huge demand in China pushes pork past chicken to roughly 36% of all meat eaten.
questions.more: Measured purely by tonnage, poultry has recently pulled close, but by share of total meat, pork leads.
questions.source: USDA AskUSDA, What is the most consumed meat in the world

questions.q: Which country produces the most olive oil, making about half the world's supply?
questions.detail: Spain produces around 1.4 million tonnes a year, close to 50% of global olive oil.
questions.why: Roughly one in every two bottles of olive oil sold worldwide comes from this one country.
questions.more: Most of it comes from the southern region of Andalusia.
questions.source: Olive Oils from Spain, Spanish Olive Oil World Leader

questions.q: Which country drinks the most tea per person each year?
questions.detail: Turkey leads at about 3.16 kg of tea per person per year, far ahead of the next country.
questions.why: The biggest tea drinkers per head aren't in Asia or Britain, but in Turkey, at over 3 kg a person.
questions.more: Ireland is second at about 2.19 kg, with the UK third at about 1.94 kg.
questions.source: World Population Review, Tea Consumption by Country 2026

questions.q: Which country grows more bananas than any other in the world?
questions.detail: India grows about 33 million tonnes of bananas a year, more than any other country by a wide margin.
questions.why: The top banana grower is India, with around a quarter of world output, but it eats almost all of them at home.
questions.more: India exports only about 1% of its bananas.
questions.source: DevelopmentAid, Top 10 banana-producing countries in the world

questions.q: India grows the most bananas but sells almost none abroad. Which country actually exports the most bananas?
questions.detail: Ecuador, growing about 6 million tonnes, is the world's largest banana exporter.
questions.why: Being the biggest grower and the biggest seller are two different jobs, and Ecuador wins the export side.
questions.more: The Philippines and Costa Rica are the next-largest exporters.
questions.source: DevelopmentAid, Top 10 banana-producing countries in the world
questions._mechanism: slicer (production vs export, routed off Q6); difficulty med

questions.q: Which country drinks the most beer per person, and has topped the list for over 30 years straight?
questions.detail: The Czech Republic drinks about 150 liters of beer per person a year, No. 1 every year since 1993.
questions.why: One country has led beer drinking per head for more than three decades, at roughly 150 liters a person.
questions.more: Austria is usually a distant second.
questions.source: Kirin Holdings, Global Beer Consumption by Country 2023

questions.q: Which country grows the most wheat in the world?
questions.detail: China produced about 140 million tonnes in 2024/25, roughly 18% of world wheat.
questions.why: The world's biggest wheat grower also eats almost all of it, so you rarely see it on export charts.
questions.more: India is the next-largest single country; the United States makes only about 7%.
questions.source: Straits Research, Top 10 Wheat-Producing Countries 2024/25

questions.q: Which country produces most of the world's vanilla, over 80% of it?
questions.detail: Madagascar supplies more than 80% of the world's natural vanilla.
questions.why: A single island nation off Africa grows the great majority of the world's vanilla beans.
questions.more: Indonesia is a distant second.
questions.source: World Population Review, Vanilla Production by Country 2026

questions.q: Which country grows over 90% of the world's saffron, the most expensive spice by weight?
questions.detail: Iran produces more than 90% of the world's saffron, much of it in Khorasan province.
questions.why: Nearly all of the world's priciest spice comes from one country in the Middle East.
questions.more: Saffron can cost $5,000 to $10,000 per pound because it's hand-picked from crocus flowers.
questions.source: FAO GIAHS, Qanat-based Saffron Farming System in Iran

questions.q: Tempura, the fried-batter dish, is famous as Japanese, but which European country's missionaries taught Japan to make it?
questions.detail: Portuguese Jesuits brought batter-frying to Japan in the 16th century; the word comes from the Latin 'tempora' (fasting days).
questions.why: A signature 'Japanese' dish actually came from European visitors about 500 years ago.
questions.more: The Portuguese also introduced the technique behind Japan's deep-fried treats during the Nanban trade.
questions.source: Wikipedia, Tempura

questions.q: Which country grows more cocoa, the bean that chocolate is made from, than any other?
questions.detail: Ivory Coast produced about 1.89 million tonnes in 2024, roughly 36% of the world's cocoa.
questions.why: The world's chocolate starts mostly in West Africa, with one country growing over a third of all cocoa.
questions.more: Neighboring Ghana is second; together the two grow nearly half the world's cocoa.
questions.source: Wikipedia, List of countries by cocoa production; World Atlas
questions._mechanism: max (reflex 'Switzerland/Belgium' make chocolate, not cocoa); difficulty med

questions.q: What is the name of the compound in chili peppers that makes them taste hot?
questions.detail: Capsaicin is the compound the Scoville scale measures; pure capsaicin rates 16,000,000 Scoville Heat Units.
questions.why: The burn isn't real heat at all, it's one chemical fooling your pain and heat receptors.
questions.more: A bell pepper scores 0 because it has almost no capsaicin.
questions.source: Wikipedia, Scoville scale

questions.q: Which country made the most wine in 2024, edging out France?
questions.detail: Italy produced about 4.4 billion liters in 2024, nearly a fifth of the world's wine, retaking the top spot.
questions.why: France and Italy trade places, and in 2024 Italy was back on top with nearly 20% of world wine.
questions.more: Italy, France, and Spain together make almost half the world's wine.
questions.source: Visual Capitalist, Ranked: Wine Production by Country

questions.q: Which U.S. state makes the most cheese, about a quarter of the country's total?
questions.detail: Wisconsin made a record 3.59 billion pounds of cheese in 2024, about 25% of U.S. output.
questions.why: One Midwestern state alone makes roughly a quarter of all American cheese.
questions.more: California is second at about 2.48 billion pounds.
questions.source: Cheese Reporter, US Cheese Production Set New Record In 2024

questions.q: Which U.S. state grows the most potatoes, roughly a third of the national crop?
questions.detail: Idaho grew about 138 million hundredweight in 2025, around a third of all U.S. potatoes.
questions.why: One state grows about one in every three American potatoes.
questions.more: Washington is second at about 87 million hundredweight.
questions.source: World Population Review, Potato Production by State 2026

questions.q: Which single topping is the most popular on pizza in the United States?
questions.detail: Pepperoni is America's favorite topping, picked by about 67% of people and found on roughly 36% of orders.
questions.why: No other topping comes close; pepperoni shows up on more than a third of all U.S. pizza orders.
questions.more: Sausage is a distant second at about 44% favorability.
questions.source: BLACK+DECKER Pizza Pulse Survey (PR Newswire, 2024)

questions.q: Despite its Scandinavian-looking name, in which country was the ice cream brand Haagen-Dazs actually created?
questions.detail: Haagen-Dazs was founded in 1961 in the Bronx, New York, by Reuben and Rose Mattus; the name is invented and means nothing in Danish.
questions.why: The fancy 'Danish' name was pure marketing for an ice cream born in New York City.
questions.more: The umlaut and 'zs' don't even follow real Danish spelling rules.
questions.source: Wikipedia, Haagen-Dazs; Atlas Obscura

questions.q: How many holes are punched into a single round Ritz cracker?
questions.detail: A Ritz cracker has 7 holes: one in the center ringed by six others, which let steam escape during baking.
questions.why: The holes aren't decoration; they let steam out so the cracker bakes flat and even, and there are exactly 7.
questions.more: A classic saltine traditionally has about 13 holes.
questions.source: Bokksu Market, Why do Ritz crackers have 7 holes
